folio.label: 52v
folio.l: That doun he gotú , and cried harrow I dye

note.notetxt: 387The ink in folio 52v is light brown.ODR

folio.l: Theise Clerkes , bete hym wele , and lete hi~ lye
And ordeyned hem , and toke here hors a non
And eke here mele , and onö here weye they gonö
And at the Mille , ©et they toke here cake
Of half a busshel flour , ful wel I bake
¢ Thus is the proud Millere , wel I bete
And hatú I lost , the gryndyngö of the whete
And paid for s s æe sopere , euäydele
Of Aleyn and of Ioún , æ beten hi~ wele
His wyf is swyued , and his dougúter als
Lo , swicú it is , a Millere to be fals
And æäfore , this ¸uerbe , is seid ful sotú
Hym thar nat wene wel , that euele dotú
A gylour , shal hi~ self , begyled by
And god æat sitteth heye , in mageste
Saue al this companye , grete and smale
Thus haue I quyt the Millere , in my tale

note.notetxt: q¶ the Reve404 in the right margin.ODR
404probably the scribe felt that the tale was not finished as the rhyme end with the direct speech of the Reeve.ODR

folio.l: ¢ Thus endetú the Reues Tale , & bigynnetú the ¸loge of the Cook
